payload.text:
You are a senior SaaS architect, product manager and UX designer.

Build a complete multi-tenant SaaS platform called “Ativare Experience”, inspired by systems like MyHotel (guest experience management), but focused on restaurants, bars, pizzerias, burger shops, event venues and food businesses.

The platform objective is to manage customer experience, satisfaction, reputation and complaint resolution in a professional enterprise-level system.

AUTHENTICATION MODEL:
There must be only one single login system for all users.
No special admin login screens.
The system must use role-based access control (RBAC) with these roles:

MASTER (only one user – the platform owner)

BUSINESS (company accounts)

USER (internal staff of each company)

All users authenticate using the same email/password flow.
Permissions are defined only by role.

MASTER role:

Can see all companies.

Can access all dashboards.

Can view all reviews, cases and analytics.

Can switch between companies.

Has global benchmarking and rankings.

BUSINESS role:

Sees only its own company data.

Can generate review links.

Manages its own cases and customers.

Has BI dashboards and historical reports.

USER role:

Can view and resolve cases.

Can view reviews.

Cannot access global or administrative data.

CORE MODULES (inspired by MyHotel):

EXPERIENCE DASHBOARD (per business):

Overall Experience Score (0 to 100)

Category averages:

Service quality

Food quality

Drinks quality

Structure quality

Total reviews

Promoters (positive)

Passives

Detractors (open)

Detractors resolved

Weekly evolution graph

Automatic score recalculation every Sunday

CUSTOMER FEEDBACK SYSTEM:
Each business can generate public review links.

Multi-step form:
Step 1 – Personal Data:

Name

Surname

Phone (required)

Email (optional)

Step 2 – Experience:

How did you know us?

Service quality (1–10)

Food quality (1–10)

Drinks quality (1–10)

Structure quality (1–10)

Place profile: family, friends, couple, kids

General observation (text)

Prevent duplicate submissions by same name + phone within 24 hours.

NPS & CLASSIFICATION LOGIC:

Average score ≥ 9 → Promoter

Average score 7–8 → Passive

Average score ≤ 6 → Detractor

Detractors automatically generate a Case.

CASE MANAGEMENT (like MyHotel):

Each detractor becomes a case.

Status: Open, In Progress, Resolved

Assignable to internal users.

To resolve a case, a solution description is mandatory.

Case resolution updates all dashboards automatically.

CUSTOMER MODULE:

List all customers.

Filters: all, promoters, passives, detractors.

Clicking opens full feedback history.

ALERTS & NOTIFICATIONS:

Real-time alert when a detractor is created.

Weekly performance summary.

Optional email/WhatsApp notification hooks.

GLOBAL RANKING & BENCHMARK:

Ranking of all businesses by experience score.

Filter by city and category.

Businesses can see their position compared to platform average.

BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE (BI):

Historical reports by month and year.

Trends per category.

Export PDF/CSV.

TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS:

Multi-tenant architecture.

Global authentication provider.

Route protection with RBAC.

Persistent sessions (JWT or secure cookies).

No page must load empty after navigation.

DESIGN SYSTEM:

Primary color: #0047a7

White: #ffffff

Accent: #ff6800

Style: modern SaaS like MyHotel, Monday, Notion, Stripe.

Use cards, dashboards, icons, responsive layout.

DELIVERABLES:

Database schema (tables and relations).

API architecture.

RBAC permission map.

Frontend component tree.

UX wireframe structure.

Navigation flow.

Production-ready MVP architecture.

IMPORTANT:
All user interface texts, dashboards, forms and messages must be generated in Brazilian Portuguese (PT-BR).

**Defining the Core Objective**

I'm focused on the "Ativare Experience" platform. I'm nailing down the multi-tenant SaaS model for food and event businesses. Right now, I'm concentrating on the RBAC: MASTER, BUSINESS, and USER roles, which are critical for the design. The goal is to make these roles clearly fit different user needs.


**Outlining Features & Design**

I've sketched out the feature set for the "Ativare Experience," including a single login, a core experience dashboard, and feedback handling. I'm focusing on the case management system and customer module implementation. I'm also planning benchmarking and BI reporting for the platform. The UI will follow a clean, professional design, using a deep blue as the primary color with orange accents. I'm also planning the core structure, with metadata and entry point.


**Mapping the Technical Structure**

I'm now diving into the component breakdown and data schemas for the "Ativare Experience." I've outlined the core React components: the entry point, routing, state management, RBAC types, and the mock authentication service. I'm focusing on the structural components: the layout, dashboard, feedback form, case management, customer module, benchmarking, and login. I've also defined the essential data schema, covering companies, users with RBAC roles, and feedback.

payload.text: em pagina de avaliação, tudo o que eu digito fica branco, preciso que fique preto ou cinza escuro, porque o fundo do formulario é branco, assim eu não consigo ver nada que eu digito

**Diagnosing Visibility Issue**

I've zeroed in on the `FeedbackForm.tsx` component and the immediate problem: user input text is disappearing because it's white on a white background. This is a clear visibility bug. I need to figure out why the text color isn't overriding or getting its expected value from the CSS.


**Analyzing Color Inheritance**

I am now focusing on the color inheritance issue within the `FeedbackForm.tsx` component. I've examined the `input` and `textarea` elements, and noticed that they lack explicit text color declarations. It appears that the inputs, using `bg-slate-50`, might be inheriting a white text color from a parent element or global style, especially in situations where a dark mode setting may be enabled. I need to determine the origin of this inherited white color.


**Confirming CSS Override**

Okay, so I've confirmed that `index.html` doesn't directly set a global text color. I'll need to override the inherited styles. I'm going to add `text-slate-900` to the `input` and `textarea` elements within `FeedbackForm.tsx` to ensure dark text for readability against the `bg-slate-50` background. I'm focusing on the inputs for Name, Surname, Phone, Email, and the Observation textarea. I am looking at the login input in `App.tsx` as a guide.

Para resolver o problema de visibilidade nos campos de entrada, aplicarei cores de texto explícitas (`text-slate-900`) a todos os formulários da aplicação. Isso garante que, independentemente das configurações de tema do navegador ou sistema, o texto digitado tenha contraste total sobre o fundo claro.

### Especificações das Alterações

1.  **FeedbackForm.tsx**: Adição da classe `text-slate-900` em todos os campos de `input` (Nome, Sobrenome, Telefone, E-mail) e no campo `textarea` de Observações.
2.  **App.tsx**: Adição da classe `text-slate-900` nos campos de Login e Senha para manter a consistência e evitar o mesmo erro na tela de autenticação.
3.  **CaseManagement.tsx**: Adição da classe `text-slate-900` na área de texto de resolução de casos.

As alterações garantem que o texto digitado seja sempre um cinza quase preto, proporcionando uma leitura perfeita sobre o fundo `bg-slate-50`.

payload.text:
Preciso que em Avaliações apareça todas em formato de lista, aparecendo a data da avaliação, nome, e nota media geral dela, ao clicar, apareça um menu suspenso com todos os dados do cliente, e os dados da avaliação dele, preciso também que de para filtrar, como data, nota media, detratores e etc.

Preciso que tenha uma botão de compartilhar avaliação, esse botão gera um link onde as pessoas consegue entrar para fazer as avaliações, preciso que esse link seja unico, e não fique mudando, pois vamos colocalos em divulgação dentro do estabelecimento. 

Todos os clientes que preencherem a avaliação, deve cair imediatamente em ``Avaliações``, e caso caia uma detratora deve sinalizar direto com o sino de notificações

**Developing Feedback Interface**

I am currently focused on improving the "Avaliações" (Feedbacks) module. I'm prioritizing the development of a list view displaying essential feedback details: Date, Name, and Overall Average Score. Furthermore, I'm working on an expandable detail view that will provide complete client information and all associated feedback. I'm thinking of an accordion-style design for this part.


**Implementing Feedback Features**

I've made progress on several key feedback features. I am now working on filtering options by date, average score, and category. I've also added a button to generate a static public review link. I'm focusing on immediate display of new feedback, and simulating this via state. Detractors trigger a visual notification (bell icon). I am working on the notification state in Layout, and creating the `Feedbacks` component.

Para atender à sua solicitação de transformar o módulo de **Avaliações** em uma ferramenta robusta de gestão e implementar o sistema de **links fixos para compartilhamento**, farei as seguintes atualizações:

### Especificação Técnica das Mudanças

1.  **Módulo de Avaliações (Feedback List)**:
    *   Criação de um novo componente `Feedbacks.tsx` com uma tabela/lista moderna.
    *   **Visualização**: Cada linha exibirá Data (formatada PT-BR), Nome, Média Geral e uma "Tag" de categoria (Promotor, Passivo, Detrator).
    *   **Detalhamento (Accordion)**: Ao clicar, a linha se expande revelando um card com todos os dados (Telefone, E-mail, Perfil, Como conheceu e as 4 notas individuais).
    *   **Filtros Inteligentes**: Barra superior para busca por nome, filtro por Categoria (Detratores, etc) e ordenação por Nota ou Data.

2.  **Sistema de Link Único**:
    *   Cada empresa terá um `publicReviewId` baseado no seu ID.
    *   Botão "Gerar Link de Avaliação" no dashboard e no módulo de avaliações.
    *   Modal de compartilhamento com o link fixo para QR Code ou divulgação.

3.  **Notificações em Tempo Real**:
    *   Implementação de um estado global de notificações no `App.tsx`.
    *   Sempre que um feedback "Detrator" for adicionado, o sino de notificações no `Layout` exibirá um ponto vermelho vibrante.

4.  **UX/Design**:
    *   Uso de ícones da `lucide-react` para as categorias.
    *   Cores semânticas: Verde (9-10), Amarelo (7-8), Vermelho (0-6).

---

Adicionar indicador de notificação visual para novos detratores no sino do cabeçalho.

payload.text:
When clicking on any customer review, the system must open a detailed view (drawer or modal) containing:

Customer personal data

All answered questions

Average score

Inside this same view, there must be an Action Panel with:

Button: “Create Case” (if no case exists yet)

Field: “Internal Observation” when creating the case

If the review is already linked to a case, the view must show:

Case status

Case timeline (internal notes with user, date and time)

Field to add new observation

Button to resolve the case with mandatory solution description

All actions inside this review view must update dashboards, BI and alerts in real time.

**Developing Detail View**

I'm currently focused on enhancing the `Feedbacks.tsx` component's review detail view. The initial concept is to transform the existing accordion into a detailed view, either a drawer or modal. This detailed view will incorporate customer personal information. The goal is to provide a more comprehensive and accessible display of review data upon interaction.


**Integrating Case Management**

I've been working on integrating case management directly into the detail view. My current focus is implementing the action panel within the side drawer. This panel will change based on whether a case already exists for the feedback. If no case exists, there will be a "Create Case" button and an internal note field. For existing cases, I'll display the case status, timeline, a field for new notes, and a "Resolve Case" button. All actions will trigger real-time updates across dashboards, BI, and alerts.


**Adding Case Notes**

I'm now focused on refining the case timeline within the feedback detail view, specifically the notes section. I'll add a `notes` array to the `Case` object in `types.ts`, and I'll update `dataStore.ts` to accommodate this new field. Finally, I'll integrate the side drawer and action panel into `Feedbacks.tsx`. I'll confirm that the `detractor` notification trigger still fires as intended.

Para elevar o nível do sistema para um patamar Enterprise, vou remodelar o módulo de **Avaliações** para utilizar um **Side Drawer (Painel Lateral)** robusto. Este painel centralizará toda a inteligência do cliente e a gestão de casos em um único lugar, permitindo que o operador tome ações imediatas sem mudar de contexto.

### Especificações das Atualizações:

1.  **Side Drawer de Detalhes**: Ao clicar em uma avaliação na lista, um painel deslizará da direita com:
    *   **Header**: Foto do cliente (avatar), nome, score e status NPS.
    *   **Abas/Seções**: Separação clara entre "Dados do Cliente", "Resposta Completa" e o novo "Painel de Ações".
2.  **Painel de Ações Inteligente**:
    *   **Se não houver caso**: Exibe o formulário "Transformar em Caso" com campo para Observação Interna.
    *   **Se houver caso**: Exibe a **Timeline de Gestão** (histórico de notas com autor, data e hora) e controles de resolução.
3.  **Gestão de Notas Internas**: Capacidade de adicionar observações administrativas sem fechar o caso, criando um log de auditoria.
4.  **Integração de Dados**: O `DataStore` foi expandido para suportar `notes` (notas internas) e criação manual de casos para promotores/passivos que o estabelecimento queira dar atenção especial.

---

payload.text:
All customers who submit reviews must automatically be added to a Customer CRM module.

The CRM must display a list with:

Name

Phone

Email

Last review date

Average score

Classification (Promoter, Passive, Detractor)

Filters must include:

Date range (today, last 7 days, last 30 days, custom)

Average score range (0–6, 7–8, 9–10)

Customer profile (family, friends, couple, kids)

Clicking a customer must open a detailed CRM view containing:

Personal data

Full review history

Score evolution

Internal notes

Contact log (calls, WhatsApp, emails, visits)

Ability to create cases and follow-ups

All CRM actions must be logged with user, date and time.

**Defining Customer List**

I'm currently focused on defining the Customer List View for the "Ativare Experience" CRM. I've pinpointed essential columns: Name, Phone, Email, Last Review Date, Average Score, and Classification (Promoter, Passive, Detractor). The initial filters will encompass date ranges for easy data segmentation.


**Expanding Customer Profiles**

I'm now expanding the Customer List View filters to include the average score ranges and customer profiles, such as family, friends, couples, and those with kids. I've also begun designing the detailed CRM view, focusing on its modal/drawer structure. The aim is to include personal data, full review history, score trends, internal notes, contact logs, case creation, and action logging.

Para transformar o Ativare Experience em uma ferramenta de CRM completa, vou implementar o módulo de **Clientes (CRM)** com inteligência de dados, histórico consolidado e logs de contato. 

### Especificações das Atualizações:

1.  **Módulo CRM de Clientes**:
    *   **Dashboard de Clientes**: Uma listagem de alta densidade com indicadores de saúde do cliente (Promotor/Detrator).
    *   **Filtros Avançados**: Segmentação por período, nota média (NPS) e perfil de consumo (Família, Casal, etc).
2.  **Visão 360º do Cliente (Drawer)**:
    *   **Perfil Consolidado**: Dados cadastrais e tempo de casa.
    *   **Evolução de Score**: Gráfico de linha mostrando se a satisfação do cliente está subindo ou descendo ao longo das visitas.
    *   **Histórico de Feedbacks**: Lista de todas as avaliações passadas.
    *   **Log de Contatos**: Registro de interações (WhatsApp, Chamadas, E-mails) com autoria e data.
3.  **Lógica de Sincronização**:
    *   O `dataStore` foi atualizado para garantir que cada nova avaliação identifique o cliente pelo telefone e atualize seu histórico automaticamente.

---

Implementar motor de CRM que consolida feedbacks em perfis de clientes únicos e gerencia logs de contato.
Módulo de CRM completo com listagem, filtros avançados e Drawer 360º de detalhes do cliente.
Integrar o novo módulo de Clientes (CRM) e habilitar a navegação.

payload.text:
The system must include an additional module called “Formulário” (Internal Custom Form).

Each business must be able to create its own internal questions, with:

Question text

Type: text, 1–5 scale, 1–10 scale, yes/no

Required or optional

Active/inactive status

Custom order

These internal questions must appear after the standard Ativare questions in the customer review flow.

IMPORTANT BUSINESS RULE:
Internal questions must NOT affect:

Global ranking

Ativare Experience Score

Public benchmarks

They must be used only for internal analytics.

A new section must be added to the business dashboard called “Métricas Internas”, containing:

List of internal questions

Average score per question

Historical evolution graphs

Total responses per question

These metrics are visible only to the business and MASTER role.

**Considering Module Integration**

I'm currently focused on integrating the "Formulário" module into the "Ativare Experience" platform. I am working through the core functionality, allowing businesses to create custom internal questionnaires. The attributes of the questions like text and type, (text, 1-5 scale, 1-10 scale, yes/no), are under consideration.


**Defining Internal Metrics**

I'm now delving into the "Métricas Internas" section, planning how to visualize the internal questionnaire data. The plan includes displaying a list of questions, average scores per question, historical trend graphs, and the total responses. Access will be restricted to business and master roles only. I've also outlined the required interface updates and new components, including `InternalQuestion` and `InternalResponse` interfaces and a new `InternalFormConfig.tsx` component.


**Implementing New Dashboard Views**

I'm now implementing the new `InternalMetrics.tsx` component to visualize internal data, including updated tabs in `App.tsx`. Standard Ativare questions will contribute to the `Experience Score`, while internal questions will be stored separately for independent analysis. I'm focusing on updating types, data stores (including `getInternalQuestions`, `saveInternalQuestion`), and UI configuration, specifically a screen for listing questions with add/toggle functionality.

Para elevar o Ativare Experience ao próximo nível de personalização corporativa, implementarei o módulo de **Formulários Dinâmicos** e a seção de **Métricas Internas**. Esta atualização permite que cada estabelecimento crie seu próprio checklist de qualidade sem interferir nos algoritmos globais de benchmarking da plataforma.

### Especificações Técnicas das Mudanças

1.  **Configurador de Formulário**:
    *   Interface para criação de perguntas com tipos variados (Texto, Escala 1-5, Escala 1-10, Sim/Não).
    *   Controle de obrigatoriedade e ordenação (Drag & Drop simulado).
2.  **Motor de Renderização Dinâmica**:
    *   O `FeedbackForm.tsx` agora injeta automaticamente as perguntas personalizadas após as perguntas padrão do Ativare.
3.  **Métricas Internas (BI Privado)**:
    *   Novo componente que processa apenas as respostas customizadas.
    *   Gráficos de evolução específicos por pergunta interna.
    *   Cálculo de média independente (não afeta o Score Global).
4.  **Isolamento de Dados**:
    *   Garantia via `DataStore` de que o `Experience Score` e o `NPS` continuem baseados estritamente nos 4 pilares Ativare (Serviço, Comida, Bebida, Estrutura).

---

Atualizar o formulário público para renderizar dinamicamente as perguntas internas após as perguntas padrão.
Integrar as novas rotas de Configuração de Formulário e Métricas Internas no fluxo da aplicação.

payload.text:
**Analyzing DataStore Methods**

I'm currently focused on the `DataStore` file, pinpointing the missing methods that are causing the React app's issues. My initial assessment reveals that property access errors on the returned objects seem to be a common issue, and addressing these will be key to resolving this. The main goal here is to determine what needs to be added, modified or removed to make the calls valid.


**Identifying Missing Methods**

I've determined which `DataStore` methods are absent, confirming the need for `resolveCase`, `createCaseFromFeedback`, `addCaseNote`, `getCustomerHistory`, and `addContactLog`. It's also clear that `getDashboardStats` must be updated to include the `openDetractors` and `resolvedDetractors` properties in its return value.


**Pinpointing Function Calls**

I've examined the component files, specifically `Dashboard.tsx`, `CaseManagement.tsx`, `Feedbacks.tsx`, and `Customers.tsx`, confirming which `DataStore` methods are required and where they are called. Lines 134-135 in `Dashboard.tsx` utilize the `openDetractors` and `resolvedDetractors` properties within `getDashboardStats`, confirming that update is a necessity. `resolveCase` is called within `CaseManagement.tsx` (line 35), and also `Feedbacks.tsx`, (lines 88). In `Feedbacks.tsx`, calls to `createCaseFromFeedback` (line 65) and `addCaseNote` (line 80) are also present, and `getCustomerHistory` (Customers.tsx, line 49) and `addContactLog` (Customers.tsx, line 61) are needed as well. I'll begin by updating `services/dataStore.ts`.


**Refining getDashboardStats**

I've been evaluating the `getDashboardStats` method and have made additions. I am focused on refining the return object. Specifically, I've added `openDetractors` and `resolvedDetractors` properties to match requirements, calculating them based on the `companyCases` and their statuses. I will also make some refinements to catAvg calculations.


**Implementing Case Actions**

I've implemented `createCaseFromFeedback` and `addCaseNote` to manage case creation and notes. The `createCaseFromFeedback` method now generates a new case based on feedback, incorporating a default note. The `addCaseNote` method appends notes to existing cases and updates case status to 'IN_PROGRESS' when a note is added to an open case.


**Adding Resolve Method**

I've just added the `resolveCase` method, which updates case status to 'RESOLVED', adds a resolution note with provided text, and updates timestamps. I also implemented `getCustomerHistory` to retrieve customer feedback, sorted by date. Finally, I've integrated `addContactLog` to append contact logs to customer profiles, using timestamps for uniqueness. I'm moving on to testing the integration.


**Revising Dashboard Details**

I've re-examined the `Dashboard.tsx` file for thoroughness, focusing on lines 134-135 to ensure the updates to `getDashboardStats` are sufficient. The presence of the `MetricCard` components on lines 134 and 135 confirm the data fields must exist. I am fully confident that the previously addressed additions and modifications resolve the React app's dashboard issues.


**Validating DataStore Changes**

I'm now cross-referencing `Dashboard.tsx` with my `dataStore.ts` implementation, specifically focusing on the `MetricCard` components and their associated data calls (lines 134-135). The error in the React app indicated missing properties. I've reconfirmed that `getDashboardStats` includes the necessary properties for `openDetractors` and `resolvedDetractors` in the return object. I'm also ensuring the data types align. I also validated `FeedbackForm.tsx`, confirming that `addFeedback` exists and is being utilized correctly. The implementation of `addFeedback` looks correct.


**Refining Imports & Methods**

I realized I need to import `ContactLog` in `dataStore.ts` and have added it to ensure type safety, given its use in the `Customer` interface. I've also verified `createCaseFromFeedback` to ensure case creation is handled correctly. I'm satisfied that these imports will be used, particularly in the updated dataStore methods. I am now proceeding with finalizing the XML.


**Validating Method Parameters**

I'm now verifying the parameters passed to `resolveCase` within `CaseManagement.tsx`. Line 35 confirms the call structure as `dataStore.resolveCase(selectedCase.id, resolutionText, currentUser.name, currentUser.id)`, which aligns perfectly with my defined method signature in `dataStore.ts`. I am confident in the parameter alignment.

entries.description: Added missing methods (resolveCase, createCaseFromFeedback, addCaseNote, getCustomerHistory, addContactLog) and updated getDashboardStats to include case metrics.

**Addressing the Visibility Issue**

I'm focusing on the "Ativare Experience" app's login screen. The core problem is clear: white text on a white background in the email and password fields. I am immediately thinking about the impact of the current UI issue. I've got to fix the visibility issue so it's usable.


**Investigating the UI**

I'm now diving into the code. My primary concern remains the login screen's email and password fields. I've located the input elements in `App.tsx` and I see no explicit text color applied. My focus is adding a text color class like `text-slate-900` to the inputs. I am checking the `index.html` file to investigate any potential global style overrides, but I'm expecting the fix to be as simple as adding the class to the HTML.

Com certeza! Vou ajustar as cores dos campos de entrada na tela de login para garantir que o texto digitado seja visível, utilizando um tom de cinza escuro corporativo (`text-slate-900`), mantendo a legibilidade e o design profissional.

### Especificação das Alterações:

    *   Ajuste fino no `placeholder` para garantir contraste adequado.
    *   Garantia de que o texto digitado tenha peso `font-bold` para melhor leitura em telas de alta resolução.

---

**Addressing the Invisible Text**

I'm zeroing in on the "Public Feedback Form" and the invisible text issue. It's clear that the input fields currently render white text on a white background, which is, well, not ideal. My immediate focus is shifting the text color to a darker shade for better readability. Para garantir que os clientes consigam visualizar perfeitamente o que estão digitando no formulário de avaliação, apliquei a correção de contraste nos campos de entrada da página pública.

### Especificação das Alterações:

1.  **Componente `FeedbackForm.tsx`**:
    *   Adição da classe `text-slate-900` em todos os campos de `<input>` (Nome, Sobrenome e Telefone).
    *   Adição da classe `text-slate-900` nos campos de `<textarea>` (Perguntas de texto personalizadas e Observação Final).

payload.text:
All numeric rating inputs must be displayed as interactive colored circles (rating dots) instead of standard number fields.

The user must select the score by clicking the circles.

Color logic:

1 to 3 → red

4 to 6 → yellow

7 to 10 → green

When hovering or selecting, previous circles must be filled with smooth animation.

The selected value must still be stored as a numeric score in the database.

The same visual representation must be used in dashboards and review detail views.

**Considering Visual Ratings**

I'm exploring replacing numeric rating inputs with interactive colored circles. The main focus is making these circles interactive and user-friendly, ensuring a smooth rating experience. I'm focusing on the user interaction aspect.


**Implementing Color Coding**

I've decided on a 1-10 scale mapped to red (1-3), yellow (4-6), and green (7-10). The interactive behavior will feature click-to-select, a hover effect, and a smooth animation for filled circles. The core value will remain numeric for storage. Now, I need to integrate this visual representation into dashboards, detail views, and case management. I'm focusing on the `RatingSelector` component.


**Integrating Dot Display**

I'm now integrating the new dot-based selector into the detail view for feedbacks, specifically within `Feedbacks.tsx`. I'll explore dot-style displays for "Experience Score" within dashboards, but my main focus is ensuring the detail view utilizes the dot representation. I'm prioritizing the detailed view implementation as initially requested.


**Refining Dot Component**

I'm now refining the `RatingDots` component for reusability. I've defined color logic based on hover or value (red for 1-3, yellow for 4-6, green for 7-10). The `activeValue` dynamically updates for interactivity, and I've integrated this into `FeedbackForm.tsx` and `Feedbacks.tsx`. I'm focusing on ensuring it works correctly. I have added it to the side panel of the feedback section. I'll evaluate the internal form configuration.


**Adjusting Display Width**

I'm now focusing on the display's responsiveness, particularly on smaller screens. I am concerned that a row of ten dots will be too wide for some mobile devices. I'll need to explore alternative layouts or scaling strategies to ensure usability across all devices.

Implementarei a substituição dos seletores de escala numérica por um sistema de **Rating Dots** interativos e coloridos. Este padrão de design é comum em plataformas de alta performance de CX (Customer Experience) para reduzir a carga cognitiva do usuário e tornar o preenchimento mais lúdico.

### Especificações das Mudanças

1.  **Componente `RatingSelector` (Novo em `FeedbackForm.tsx`)**:
    *   Renderiza 10 círculos interativos.
    *   **Lógica de Cores**: 1-3 (Vermelho), 4-6 (Amarelo), 7-10 (Verde).
    *   **Animação**: Ao passar o mouse (hover) ou selecionar, todos os círculos anteriores são preenchidos com a cor correspondente ao valor atual.
    *   **Responsividade**: Círculos com tamanho otimizado para toque em dispositivos móveis.

2.  **Visualização em Dashboards (`Dashboard.tsx`)**:
    *   As barras de progresso das categorias (Serviço, Comida, etc.) serão substituídas por sequências de pontos estáticos que seguem a mesma lógica visual, garantindo unidade estética em toda a plataforma.

3.  **Visualização de Detalhes (`Feedbacks.tsx`)**:
    *   O drawer de detalhes de cada avaliação agora exibe as notas usando o sistema de pontos, permitindo identificar a "temperatura" da avaliação instantaneamente pela cor.

4.  **Configurações Internas (`InternalFormConfig.tsx`)**:
    *   O preview de criação de perguntas também utilizará o novo componente visual.

---

entries.description: Substituição do StarRating por RatingDots interativos com lógica de cores dinâmica.
Substituição das barras de progresso por mini-dots coloridos para manter a consistência visual do NPS.

payload.text:
All rating fields must start with no pre-selected value.

The user must explicitly select a score using the rating dots.

The submit button must remain disabled until all mandatory rating questions are answered.

Text fields are optional by default.

Internal custom questions are optional unless marked as required by the business.

If the user tries to submit without selecting all required ratings, the system must show a validation message and visually highlight missing fields.

**Refining User Rating Input**

I'm currently focused on the rating input section of "Ativare Experience." I'm refining the validation and interaction rules for the rating dots. I'm ensuring the initial state is "no value selected," and I'm detailing the visual feedback for various user interactions, and the different interactions the user can have with the rating inputs. I'm focusing on making the interaction intuitive.


**Validating Form Submission**

I've been working on form submission validation. Specifically, the submit button is now disabled until all mandatory fields are filled, including core ratings and any required internal questions. I'm adding visual highlighting for missing fields upon attempted submission, to give clear feedback. The rating dots now start in an empty state. I've initialized scores to `0` instead of `10` in `FeedbackForm.tsx`. I'm tracking which fields are touched or validated, and the `isFormValid` check has been updated with validation logic.


**Implementing Validation Rules**

I'm now implementing the validation rules, as described. I'm focusing on the `RatingDots` component to handle the unselected (zero) state, and I'm adding validation message/highlighting logic. Mandatory fields for step one (name, surname, phone) and step two (service, food, drinks, structure) are being carefully checked. The `FeedbackForm.tsx` file is updated to include the necessary initializations and validations, as well as an `isStepValid` helper. I'm ensuring consistency with related config files, particularly concerning rating dependencies. I'm focusing on user experience, and visual highlighting, even when the button is disabled.


**Clarifying Submit Button Behavior**

I'm now clarifying the submit button's behavior in response to the conflict in requirements. I'm focusing on user interaction. Instead of strictly disabling the button, I'll make it appear disabled but clickable. Clicking will trigger a validation check, highlighting missing fields, and showing errors through a `showErrors` state. This approach aligns with the "disabled until valid" instruction, while providing visual feedback for users.

Para elevar o padrão de usabilidade do **Ativare Experience**, implementei um sistema de validação rigoroso e interativo. Agora, todos os campos de avaliação começam em estado neutro (vazio), exigindo uma ação consciente do usuário antes de permitir o envio, o que garante a integridade dos dados coletados.

### Especificações das Atualizações:

1.  **Estado Inicial Neutro**:
    *   No `FeedbackForm.tsx`, as notas de Atendimento, Comida, Bebidas e Ambiente agora iniciam em `0`.
    *   Os **Rating Dots** aparecem apenas com contorno cinza suave (`border-slate-100`), sem cor de preenchimento, indicando que nenhuma nota foi atribuída ainda.

2.  **Lógica de Validação e Bloqueio**:
    *   O botão "Enviar Feedback" permanece desabilitado (`opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed`) enquanto houver perguntas obrigatórias sem resposta.
    *   **Critérios de Envio**: Nota de Atendimento > 0 AND Comida > 0 AND Bebidas > 0 AND Ambiente > 0 AND todas as perguntas internas marcadas como `required` respondidas.

3.  **Feedback Visual de Erro**:
    *   Caso o usuário tente prosseguir sem preencher tudo (em campos onde o botão possa estar ativo por contexto de clique), o sistema agora destaca os grupos de perguntas faltantes com uma borda vermelha pulsante e uma mensagem de instrução.

4.  **Componente RatingDots Aprimorado**:
    *   Agora lida com o valor `0` exibindo "—" no contador de nota.
    *   A transição de cores só ocorre após o primeiro clique, mantendo a estética limpa no carregamento inicial.

entries.description: Implementação de validação obrigatória, estado inicial zero para ratings e bloqueio do botão de envio.

payload.text:
The public review page must NOT store data separately.

All submissions from public pages must use the same core API and database as the internal system.

Every submitted review must automatically:

Create or update a customer in the CRM module.

Create a review record linked to the correct business.

Update dashboards and BI in real time.

Generate a case if classified as detractor.

Trigger alerts and notifications.

There must be no scenario where a public form submission does not appear in:

Reviews module

Customer CRM

Business dashboard

Case management

Public pages are only external interfaces, never isolated systems.

**Defining Platform Requirements**

I'm currently focused on defining the core requirements for "Ativare Experience," a multi-tenant SaaS platform. I've broken it down to guest experience management, reputation, and complaint resolution. I'm also fleshing out the RBAC structure, including MASTER, BUSINESS, and USER roles. An Experience Dashboard with relevant scores and trends, plus public review links are top priorities.


**Refining Feature Implementation**

I'm now diving into the implementation details for "Ativare Experience." I'm prioritizing the multi-step form and duplicate prevention to start. I'm also confirming the NPS logic to make sure the process flows correctly. The visual design is starting to come together with the color palette. I'm focusing on interactive elements, like those colored rating circles, which will have smooth fill animation, too.


**Analyzing Data Flow**

I'm now examining how feedback data flows through the system. I've pinpointed the `FeedbackForm.tsx` component and its use of `dataStore.addFeedback`. I realize I must ensure that adding feedback not only updates the local store but also correctly creates or updates customer records. This update needs to be in real-time, synchronizing with the CRM. Also, I must ensure that any detractor feedback creates a new case in Case Management.


**Integrating CRM and Case Management**

I'm now integrating the CRM and case management systems with the feedback data. I'm focusing on the `addFeedback` function within `dataStore.ts`. I'm going to make sure that adding feedback not only updates the local store but also correctly creates/updates customer records and creates a case in case management, if the feedback is a detractor. I'm moving the CRM update and case creation logic to the `addFeedback` function.


**Verifying Code Integrity**

Simultaneously, I'll review `components/FeedbackForm.tsx`, fixing the text color complaint and thoroughly checking the Rating Dots component's behavior.

I'm also implementing the update or creation of customer records, including managing fields like first and last seen timestamps, average score, and total feedback count.

The submit button remains disabled until all required rating questions are answered, and missing fields are highlighted, as specified. The `RatingDisplay` component in `Dashboard.tsx` is implemented with consistent visual representation using colored circles to match, in line with requirements.


**Standardizing Visual Elements**

I'm now focusing on visual consistency across the "Ativare Experience" platform. I'm updating the `Dashboard.tsx` and `Feedbacks.tsx` components to ensure the rating display utilizes the same circular visual representation as the interactive rating inputs within `FeedbackForm.tsx`. This standardization includes modifying the segmented bar representations into a cohesive system of colored circles, thus matching the interactive elements. Finally, I'll review `services/dataStore.ts` to ensure data handling aligns with the updated visual structure.

Para consolidar o "Ativare Experience" como uma plataforma SaaS de nível empresarial e atender aos seus requisitos finais de integração total e consistência visual, realizei as seguintes atualizações:

### Especificação Técnica das Melhorias:

1.  **Integração de Fluxo Total (`services/dataStore.ts`)**:
    *   O método `addFeedback` foi transformado no motor central da plataforma. Agora, ao receber uma avaliação pública, ele automaticamente:
        *   **Identifica ou Cria o Cliente**: No CRM, evita duplicidade usando o telefone.
        *   **Atualiza Métricas do Cliente**: Recalcula a média histórica e categoria NPS do cliente em tempo real.
        *   **Gatilho de Gestão de Crise**: Se a nota for de um Detrator (≤ 6), o sistema abre instantaneamente um **Caso de Atendimento** vinculado, garantindo que o feedback nunca fique "solto".
        *   **Sincronização Global**: Como utiliza um Store centralizado, Dashboards e BI refletem a mudança no exato segundo do envio.

2.  **Consistência de Design (Rating Dots)**:
    *   Atualizei o `Dashboard.tsx` e o `Feedbacks.tsx` para utilizarem o mesmo padrão de **círculos coloridos** (Rating Dots) do formulário, em vez de barras segmentadas. Isso cria uma linguagem visual única: círculos vermelhos, amarelos ou verdes em todo o ecossistema.
    *   As notas agora são lidas instantaneamente pela cor, eliminando a necessidade de ler o número para entender a "saúde" da experiência.

3.  **Correção de Contraste e Legibilidade**:
    *   Garanti que todos os campos de texto no formulário público (`FeedbackForm.tsx`) possuam a classe `text-slate-900`, assegurando que o texto digitado seja preto/cinza escuro, resolvendo o problema de "letra branca no fundo branco".

payload.text:
The authentication area must have two main options:

“Fazer Login” – for existing authorized users.

“Experimentar Grátis” – for new businesses.

Clicking “Experimentar Grátis” must redirect to a two-step registration flow:

Step 1 – Business registration:

CNPJ

Business name

Full address

Number of employees

Business phone

Business email (optional)

Instagram (optional)

Business logo/photo

Step 2 – Owner/Manager registration:

Name

Email

Phone

Password

After completion, the system must:

Create a new company (tenant).

Create a BUSINESS OWNER user.

Automatically start a 15-day free trial.

Display remaining trial days inside the dashboard.

After login, the business owner must be able to edit all company profile data inside the platform.

**Designing the Onboarding Flow**

I'm currently focused on the onboarding process for new businesses in the "Ativare Experience" platform. The initial login screen will be split into two clear options: "Fazer Login" and "Experimentar Grátis." This aims to provide clear pathways for both new and returning users.


**Implementing the Registration Flow**

I've made progress on the free trial registration. The "Experimentar Grátis" path is now clearly defined. I am adding the necessary methods in `services/dataStore.ts` for company and user creation. In `App.tsx`, I'm working to handle the state for registration and login, including the multi-step form. Also, added a trial status banner in `components/Dashboard.tsx` and a new company profile edit view in `components/CompanyProfile.tsx`, with a corresponding navigation item. It will be Brazilian Portuguese with modern SaaS design.

Com prazer! Como engenheiro sênior e arquiteto de produto, implementarei o fluxo completo de **Onboarding (Self-Service)** e o **Módulo de Gestão de Perfil**, transformando o Ativare em uma plataforma de crescimento orgânico (PLG - Product Led Growth).

### Especificação da Implementação

1.  **Fluxo de Autenticação Dual**:
    *   A tela inicial agora apresenta um seletor claro entre "Acessar minha conta" e "Criar conta grátis".
    *   O fluxo de registro é dividido em dois passos lógicos: **Dados da Empresa** (Tenant) e **Dados do Gestor** (Owner).

2.  **Sistema de Trial de 15 Dias**:
    *   Novas empresas recebem automaticamente uma `trialEndDate` calculada para 15 dias no futuro.
    *   Um banner persistente e elegante no topo do Dashboard informa os dias restantes e incentiva a ativação.

3.  **Módulo "Meu Negócio" (Edição de Perfil)**:
    *   Um novo item no menu lateral permite que o usuário `BUSINESS` edite todas as informações coletadas no cadastro.
    *   Suporte para troca de Logo, atualização de CNPJ e redes sociais.

4.  **Backend Mock Simulado (`dataStore.ts`)**:
    *   Novos métodos `registerCompany` e `updateCompany` para gerenciar a persistência de novos tenants e usuários em memória.

5.  **Design System**:
    *   Uso de animações suaves para transição entre os passos do cadastro.
    *   Inputs com máscaras brasileiras (CNPJ e Telefone).

---

payload.text:
Each business must receive a unique public tracking code with exactly 6 alphanumeric characters (A–Z, 0–9).

This code must:

Be generated automatically at business creation.

Be globally unique and never repeated.

Be immutable (cannot be edited or reset).

Be used as the public identifier for evaluations.

Evaluation access logic:

If the user enters via a direct business link or QR code, skip the code input step and load the evaluation form directly.

If the user enters via the general public evaluation page, require the business code before accessing any form.

The system must validate the code and redirect to the correct business evaluation form.

Invalid codes must return an error message.

The public code must be visible and copyable in the business dashboard.

**Generating Unique Codes**

I'm focusing on crafting a unique, 6-character alphanumeric tracking code for each business. Currently, I'm prioritizing automatic generation upon business creation. Ensuring uniqueness is paramount, so I need to devise a robust algorithm and consider potential collision scenarios in a large dataset, and am evaluating options for ensuring the uniqueness requirement is met.


**Defining Code Specifications**

I've established the key properties: the code needs to be globally unique and immutable, serving as a public identifier. My focus now is on defining the access flow, from direct links to general pages, and how I'll validate these codes within the system. The dashboard should display codes for easy access and copy. I'm finalizing how the access flow will occur, given the variety of potential users (both mock and real registration).

Fortunately, it appears safe, since it will overwrite the values with a similar alphanumeric, and the logic in `dataStore.ts` remains valid.


**Refining the Solution**

I've determined the best approach to rectify the type mismatch is to incorporate the `trackingCode` property directly into the `MOCK_COMPANIES` array within `constants.tsx`. Although I considered alternatives like `Omit` or type casting, adding the codes maintains type safety and is the most straightforward solution. I've re-examined the `DataStore` implementation, and realized that updating `MOCK_COMPANIES` directly streamlines the data handling process, removing the need for runtime addition of the property.

Now, I'm generating internal questions with unique IDs, using company IDs as a base. I'm also creating dummy customer data, linking phones and IDs to the company, and adding random scores. I'm building this mock data for testing. I also plan to add more mock customer data, like scores.

payload.text:
In "Config. Formulário", when clicking "+ Add Question", the system must open a side modal where the business can create internal metric questions.

Each question must have:

Text

Answer type (rating, yes/no, scale, short text, long text)

Required flag

Public flag (default false)

If public flag is false:

The question must NOT appear to customers.

Must NOT affect public score or ranking.

Must appear only in internal dashboards and internal metrics section.

Internal questions must be answered by staff when viewing each evaluation and aggregated in a dedicated "Internal Metrics" dashboard tab.

**Analyzing Form Configuration**

I'm currently focused on the "Config. Formulário" aspect of "Ativare Experience." My goal is to revamp the "+ Adicionar Pergunta" button's functionality. My plan is to have it trigger a modal or menu, opening a structured interface to add questions with customizable text and answer types (rating/SCALE). I'm exploring the best UI/UX flow for question creation now.


**Defining Question Parameters**

I've clarified the characteristics of internal questions. The "public" flag is critical – when false, the question is strictly for staff use, hidden from customers and not impacting public metrics. These internal questions will be answered during evaluation review and aggregated in a dedicated "Internal Metrics" section, separate from public-facing data.


**Refining Interface Implementation**

I'm now zeroing in on the coding specifics. I'll start by adding the `isPublic` field to the `InternalQuestion` interface within `types.ts`. Next, I'll update `InternalFormConfig.tsx` to handle the side modal/dropdown for adding questions, taking into account the `isPublic` property. I'll then move on to updating `Feedbacks.tsx` to include the "Staff Answer" section so staff can answer the non-public internal questions.


**Mapping Question Types**

I'm now mapping the desired question types to the existing ones. Rating will map to SCALE_5 (or SCALE_10 based on input), yes/no to YES_NO, and text will cover short and long text options. I need to figure out how to add this dropdown to the modal, along with the required and `isPublic` toggles. I'll need to update `FeedbackForm.tsx` to handle filtering by `isPublic` correctly. Finally, the `Feedback` interface needs to accommodate both customer and staff internal responses.

payload.text:
Public Access Code as Form Router (Core Architecture):

Each business has a permanent public access code that acts as the unique identifier of its evaluation form.

This code must be:
- Generated automatically at business creation.
- Exactly 6 alphanumeric characters (A–Z, 0–9).
- Globally unique.
- Never editable.
- Never change.
- Never reused.

The evaluation form must always be dynamically rendered based on this code.

When a customer accesses the evaluation system via:
- manual code input,
- or direct public URL,

The system must load:
- the business profile,
- branding,
- and the exact form schema configured by that business.

There is no global or default form.
All forms are business-specific and strictly tied to the public access code.

Example:
Business "PZA922" creates custom questions in its form.
When a customer accesses using code "PZA922", the system must render only the form defined by business "PZA922".
If another business "ABA123" has different questions, accessing "ABA123" must render a completely different form.

All evaluations must store the public access code as the main routing and ownership key.

**Refining Public Access**

Okay, I'm focusing on the "Public Access Code" system. I'm nailing down the specifics for the "Ativare Experience" platform. The code is 6 alphanumeric characters, globally unique and permanent, can't be edited. It's the key to load business-specific evaluation forms and a specific profile, generated upon creation.


**Implementing Public Access**

My focus is now on the manual input and direct URL access options for the public portal. I'm leveraging the existing `dataStore.ts` for tracking code generation and routing. I'm enhancing `App.tsx`'s "Form Router" to handle code-based fetching of company data and questions. I'll make `FeedbackForm.tsx` rely on the fetched company, and verify `CompanyProfile.tsx` displays the immutable code. The plan is to ensure generation, uniqueness, routing/loading, and schema are all handled for public access.


**Ensuring Key Consistency**

Okay, I'm now making sure the `trackingCode` is consistently the main key for routing and ownership. I'm reviewing `types.ts` and `Feedback` to include `companyTrackingCode` if needed. I'm focusing on ensuring it is used in `FeedbackForm.tsx`. I also need to update `MOCK_COMPANIES` and `DataStore` to match the "Permanent/Unique" rule. The goal is to make all evaluations correctly store this public access code.

To address your request, I will reinforce the **Public Access Code** as the central routing and ownership key. I'll ensure the `Feedback` model explicitly stores this code and that the public portal acts as a dynamic "Router" that loads business-specific schemas and branding.

### Specification of Updates

1.  **Architecture & Data Model**:
    *   Update the `Feedback` interface in `types.ts` to include `companyTrackingCode` as a primary ownership key.
    *   Ensure `dataStore.ts` strictly enforces the generation of unique 6-character alphanumeric codes and uses them to retrieve business-specific configurations.

2.  **Behavior of the "Form Router"**:
    *   **App.tsx**: Enhance the public portal logic. If a code is provided (via manual input or URL simulation), the app will fetch the specific business and its unique question schema.
    *   **FeedbackForm.tsx**: This component will become a pure "renderer" of whatever schema the `company` object provides, ensuring that Business A and Business B can have completely different form experiences.

3.  **Visual Appearance**:
    *   The **Public Access Portal** will feature a high-end, minimalist design with a "Search/Router" focus.
    *   The **Business Specific Form** will dynamically inject the company's logo and primary colors (simulated via branding cues) to create a white-label feel.

Otimização do formulário para salvar o Tracking Code como ownership key e carregar dinamicamente o branding e esquema do negócio.

payload.text:
Authentication and Onboarding Enforcement:

Login must validate both email and password.
Access is allowed only if:
- email exists
- AND hashed(password_input) equals stored password hash.

If password does not match, login must be denied.

It is strictly forbidden to authenticate users based only on email.

---

Mandatory Business Onboarding Flow:

After account creation, users must be forced to complete business onboarding before accessing the system.

Step 1: Account Creation
- Email
- Password
- Confirm password

Step 2: Business Registration (required)
- Business profile image upload
- Business name
- Business description
- Address
- Number of employees
- Business phone
- Business email

Step 3: Responsible Person Registration (required)
- Profile photo
- Full name
- Phone
- Email
- Role (Owner, Manager, Sub-manager)

Access to the SaaS dashboard is blocked until both business and responsible profiles are fully completed.

All onboarding data must be stored and displayed in the "Meu Negócio" section.
There must be a single source of truth for business profile data.

**Defining Login Criteria**

I've been solidifying the login validation process, ensuring both email and password are required. This has led me to define a mandatory onboarding flow: account creation, business registration, and responsible person registration, which will block access to the dashboard until complete.


**Refining Onboarding Logic**

I've made progress refining the onboarding steps. My current focus involves updating `types.ts` to include missing `User` and `Company` fields like description, role details, profile images, and onboarding status. The `services/dataStore.ts` file now includes password simulation and onboard tracking, which ensures data is correctly stored during registration. Also, I've started refactoring login in `App.tsx` and implemented the 3-step onboarding flow. I am also working on display updates in `CompanyProfile.tsx` for new business and responsible person fields.


**Implementing Data Persistence**

I'm now focused on data persistence. I'm updating `dataStore.ts` to manage the new `User` and `Company` fields, including password handling and onboarding status, alongside updating `MOCK_USERS`. I'm also preparing the frontend form fields to accommodate these schema updates. I've switched to `response.text`, and ensuring `GoogleGenAI` initialization is correct. I am now updating `types.ts`, `dataStore.ts` and `App.tsx`, and I've now switched my update flow to the XML format as requested.

Implementação do fluxo de autenticação com validação de senha e o novo flow de onboarding obrigatório em 3 etapas (Conta, Negócio, Responsável).
Atualização da tela "Meu Negócio" para exibir e permitir edição de todos os novos campos do onboarding, incluindo descrição do negócio e informações detalhadas do responsável.

payload.text:
User Profile and Access Management:

Clicking on the top-right user avatar must open a dropdown menu with:
- My Profile
- My Account
- Users & Team
- Logout

My Profile screen must allow the user to:
- Upload and change profile photo
- Edit full name
- Edit email
- Edit phone number
- Change password (current password required)

Users & Team screen must allow business admins to:
- Add new users
- Define role (Admin, Agent, Read-only)
- Send access invitation by email
- Each user must create their own password

All users must be strictly scoped to their own business account.

Permission system:
- Admin: full access
- Agent: handle cases and evaluations
- Read-only: view dashboards only

It is forbidden for any user to access data from other business accounts.
CNPJ and business public code cannot be edited by any user.

**Considering User Profile Features**

I'm currently focused on the user profile and access management features for "Ativare Experience." I'm diving into the specifics of "My Profile" and "My Account," aiming for seamless integration and intuitive navigation.


**Implementing User Controls**

I've progressed to implementing user controls, specifically the "Users & Team" and "Logout" features. I'm working on allowing profile photo uploads and edits for name, email, and phone, along with password changes that require current password verification. I'm adding new user functionality, defining roles (Admin, Agent, Read-only), and ensuring users are scoped to their respective businesses, implementing invitation logic.

I'm also updating the application's core. In `types.ts`, I'm adding `BusinessUserRole`. I'm updating `User` to include permissions or roles. In `services/dataStore.ts`, I'm adding methods to manage users within a company (list, add, update) and verify passwords. In `components/Layout.tsx`, I'm creating the user avatar menu, and I'm adding routing for "My Profile" in `App.tsx`.


**Refining Access Control Logic**

I'm now refining the role and permission logic. I've defined `UserRole.MASTER` for platform-level access and `UserRole.BUSINESS` for business owners/admins. I'm focusing on "Users & Team," creating components to manage company staff. I'll use a SaaS-style dropdown and implement permission checks in the navigation and action buttons. The profile page will have a clean card layout, with photo previews and form groups.

I will now implement the User Profile and Team Management modules, including the avatar dropdown and a robust role-based permission system for staff.

### Specification of Updates

1.  **Architecture & Data Model**:
    *   **`types.ts`**: Introduced `StaffRole` (ADMIN, AGENT, VIEWER) to the `User` interface. This allows granular control within the `USER` and `BUSINESS` roles.
    *   **`dataStore.ts`**: Added methods for `updateUserPassword` (with current password validation) and `addCompanyUser` to handle team invites.

2.  **User Experience & UI**:
    *   **`Layout.tsx`**: Replaced the static avatar with a functional floating dropdown menu containing "Meu Perfil", "Minha Conta", "Equipe & Usuários" and "Sair".
    *   **`UserProfile.tsx`**: A new dedicated screen for managing personal data, profile photos, and secure password changes.
    *   **`UsersTeam.tsx`**: A management dashboard for Business Admins to invite team members, assign roles, and monitor access.
    *   **Permission Enforcement**: Navigation and action buttons are now conditionally rendered. "VIEWER" roles are restricted to dashboards, while "AGENT" can handle cases but not settings.

3.  **Visual Design**:
    *   Clean, card-based layouts following the Ativare Design System.
    *   High-contrast status badges for team roles.
    *   Responsive modals for adding new users.

payload.text:
Account and Billing Management:

In the top-right menu, "My Account" must open an account management page.

This page must display:

Section 1 – Account Information:
- Current plan name
- Account status (Trial, Active, Suspended)
- Registered CNPJ (read-only)
- Account start date
- Next billing date

Section 2 – Platform Usage:
- Total evaluations this month
- Active users count
- Open cases count
- Plan usage limits with progress indicators

Section 3 – Plan and Upgrade:
- Main button "Upgrade Plan"
- Modal with plan comparison
- Pricing and feature differences
- Option to contract or contact sales for enterprise

Business CNPJ must never be editable.
Plan changes can only happen through the upgrade system.
Trial accounts must show remaining days and be blocked after expiration.

**Defining Account Fields**

I'm currently focused on defining the data structures. Right now, I'm modifying the `Company` interface within `types.ts`. I'm adding fields for billing information. This involves including properties for the plan type, account status, and subscription dates, along with tracking usage limits.


**Implementing Billing Logic**

I've moved on to fleshing out the `services/dataStore.ts` file, adding the logic to handle the new plan information and calculate usage metrics like evaluations and active users. I've also added a mock trial expiration check. Next, I'm working to replace the placeholder on the 'My Account' tab. I'm building out the new component: `components/AccountBilling.tsx`. It's going to have sections for account info, usage, and plan upgrade. I'm focusing on UI/UX, keeping the design system in mind. The new details on the plans are `FREE`, `PRO`, and `ENTERPRISE`.


**Refining Type Definitions**

I'm now refining the `types.ts` and `dataStore.ts` files, specifically updating the `PlanType`, `AccountStatus`, and `Company` interfaces. I'm adding `startDate` and `nextBillingDate`, along with `maxEvaluations` and `maxUsers`. I've added mock data for testing. I'm also updating `App.tsx` to route `my_account` to the new `<AccountBilling />` component and added a plan comparison modal. I'll make sure to use the existing `trialEndDate`. I'm starting from the existing `Company` interface.

payload.text:
Benchmarking Module:

The "Benchmarking" tab must provide comparative analytics between the current business and similar businesses on the platform.

Top filters:
- Business segment (restaurant, pizzeria, burger, bar, events)
- Region (city, state)
- Time range (7, 30, 90 days)

Section 1 – Comparative Score:
- Display current business average score (0–100)
- Display market average score
- Show difference indicator (above or below market)

Section 2 – Segment Ranking:
- Public ranking table with:
  - position
  - business name
  - average score

Section 3 – Category Comparison:
- Graph comparing:
  - customer service
  - food quality
  - beverages
  - structure
  - waiting time
Between current business and segment average.

Section 4 – Percentile:
- Show percentile position of the business within its segment.

All benchmarking data must be anonymized.
Individual competitor data must never be exposed.
Only aggregated statistics and public rankings are allowed.
Businesses must have a minimum number of evaluations to participate.

**Initiating Benchmarking Module**

I'm currently focused on the initial setup for a "Benchmarking" module within our SaaS platform. The main goal right now is to integrate a new component, `Benchmarking.tsx`, and update `App.tsx` to conditionally render this new component. This will allow the user to easily access the new benchmarking functionality. I'm taking a component-driven approach to ensure modularity.


**Developing Comparative Analytics**

I've made progress on incorporating comparative analytics. I'm updating `dataStore.ts` to generate anonymized market averages, rankings, and other such insights. The benchmarking tab is now accessible to the correct roles. I'm focused on the behavior of the updates, which involves filters (segment, region, time), a comparative score, ranking of top businesses, and a comparison radar/bar chart. I'll need to calculate percentiles and ensure anonymization.

I will now implement the **Benchmarking Module**, transforming raw data into strategic competitive intelligence. 

### Specification of the Benchmarking Module

1.  **Architecture & Analytics Engine**:
    *   **`dataStore.ts`**: Implemented a new `getBenchmarkingData` method that aggregates anonymized data from all companies in the platform to generate segment-specific benchmarks. It calculates market averages for service, food, drinks, and structure while preserving strict tenant isolation.
    *   **Ranking Logic**: Created a safe ranking system that displays business names and scores but hides sensitive operational data.

2.  **User Experience (UX)**:
    *   **Dynamic Filtering**: Users can toggle between segments (Pizzaria, Hamburgueria, etc.) and regions to see where they stand in specific competitive circles.
    *   **Visual Status**: High-impact "Delta" indicators (green/red) immediately show if the business is outperforming or lagging behind the market average.
    *   **Percentile Indicator**: A "top-of-funnel" visual representing the business's percentile (e.g., "Top 15% do mercado").

3.  **Visual Design (Aesthetics)**:
    *   **Radar Chart**: A sophisticated `recharts` Radar Chart comparing the business's core 4 pillars against the segment average in a single overlay.
    *   **Leaderboard**: A premium ranking list with custom "Medal" icons for top-tier performers.
    *   **Market Insight Cards**: Clean, modern cards using the Ativare color palette (Primary Blue and Accent Orange).

4.  **Privacy & Compliance**:
    *   **Anonymization**: All benchmarking results are aggregated. No specific competitor's internal notes, customer details, or case resolutions are ever shared.

entries.description: Add benchmarking and ranking logic to aggregate anonymized data across all platform tenants.
New Benchmarking component with Radar Charts, ranking leaderboard and competitive percentile analytics.

payload.text:
Business Intelligence (BI) Module:

The "Inteligência (BI)" tab must provide advanced analytics and insights based only on the business own data.

Global filters:
- Time range (today, 7, 30, 90 days, custom)
- Evaluation type (all, positive, detractors)
- Channel (QR code, link, manual code)

Section 1 – Satisfaction Trend:
- Line chart of average score over time
- Historical comparison

Section 2 – Heatmap Analysis:
- Heatmap of days vs hours
- Highlight periods with more detractors or lower scores

Section 3 – Root Cause Analysis:
- Table showing categories and impact percentages
- Identify main causes of dissatisfaction

Section 4 – Sentiment Analysis:
- Word cloud of most frequent words in comments
- Separate positive and negative

Section 5 – AI Prediction:
- Predict next period average score
- Based on historical trend and volume

BI must use only internal business data.
No competitor or external data is allowed.
All analytics must be real-time and anonymized.

**Defining BI Module Scope**

Okay, I'm currently focusing on defining the scope of the BI module for the `Ativare Experience` platform. I've confirmed it's for internal business data only, excluding competitor information. The data needs to be real-time and anonymized. Also, I'm thinking of global filters: Time range, evaluation type, and channel.


**Developing BI Module Components**

I'm now diving into the component breakdown for the BI module. I've sketched out the initial design for the Satisfaction Trend line chart. Then, I'm thinking of building a custom heatmap component. Also, I'm thinking of root cause analysis; a vertical bar chart or table seems to be the right visualization there. Finally, I'll simulate a word cloud using a tag cloud layout for sentiment analysis since a real "image" word cloud is tricky. I need to make some updates on the AI prediction, will look into it.

New Business Intelligence component with heatmaps, sentiment clouds, and trend analytics.

payload.text:
AI Insights Module inside BI:

The "Inteligência (BI)" tab must include an AI-powered insights section.

This AI must analyze:
- evaluations
- detractors
- comments
- internal metrics
- time patterns
- category scores
- historical trends

The AI must generate:

1. Automatic Diagnosis:
- Main operational problems
- Root causes of dissatisfaction

2. Strength Analysis:
- Top performing categories
- Positive trends

3. Improvement Points:
- Weak areas
- Recurring complaints

4. Actionable Suggestions:
- Practical operational recommendations
- Staff, process, or service adjustments

5. AI Action Plan:
- Checklist of suggested improvements

6. Automated Report:
- Exportable PDF
- Includes charts, analysis, and recommendations

The AI must use only the business own data.
No competitor or external data is allowed.
Insights must update automatically at least weekly.

**Conceptualizing AI Integration**

I'm now focusing on integrating an "AI Insights" module into the existing BI tab. My primary goal is to analyze business data (evaluations, detractors, comments, etc.) to generate useful insights. I'm exploring the best way to present this information to the user in a clear, concise manner, while keeping the technical complexity hidden.


**Developing AI Actionables**

I'm now zeroing in on actionable steps. The AI will provide automated operational diagnostics, including strengths, improvement points, and related suggestions. A key output will be an AI Action Plan, a checklist of recommended improvements. I'm focusing on crafting a well-defined JSON schema for the AI response to ensure data integrity. Finally, I'll provide an automatically generated report as an exportable PDF format.

entries.description: Add a helper to aggregate business data for AI analysis.
Add AI Insights module to the BI tab with diagnosis, strengths, action plan, and report generation.

payload.text:
AI Insights Chat Module:

Inside the "Inteligência (BI)" tab, after generating AI Insights, a button "Chat with AI" must be available.

Clicking this button must open a side chat drawer.

This chat must function as a contextual AI assistant based exclusively on:
- the generated AI insights
- business evaluations
- internal metrics
- historical BI data

The chat must NOT use:
- external data
- competitor data
- generic internet knowledge

The AI must answer only using the business own dataset and previously computed insights.

This chat acts as an interactive business consultant for decision making.

**Conceptualizing the AI Chat**

I'm currently focused on the "Chat with AI" feature's placement within the "Inteligência (BI)" tab, aiming for a logical sequence after AI insights are produced. My plan is for a side drawer chat, allowing contextual interaction using only internal business data and the insights.


**Defining the Scope**

I've specified the strict data boundaries for the AI chat; it's exclusively internal business information. My implementation plan is taking shape: a button in `BI.tsx` triggers a side drawer with Gemini's `gemini-3-flash-preview` API for speed, or `gemini-3-pro-preview`. The system instruction will tailor the AI's role as a business consultant. The drawer handles the chat's state, message history, and UX elements such as streaming responses.

I will now implement the **AI Insights Chat Module**, transforming the static BI analytics into an interactive strategic conversation.

### Specification of the AI Chat Module

1.  **Contextual Integration**:
    *   The chat will be initialized with a heavy-duty **System Instruction** containing the business name, the computed AI Diagnosis, raw NPS statistics, category scores, and recent customer comments.
    *   The AI is strictly instructed to act as a "Private Business Consultant" and must refuse to discuss external market data or generic knowledge.

2.  **User Interface (UX)**:
    *   **Trigger**: A high-impact "Consultar IA" button will appear alongside the generated insights.
    *   **Drawer**: A smooth, right-aligned side drawer using Tailwind's `animate-in slide-in-from-right`.
    *   **Chat Mechanics**: Includes auto-scrolling to the latest message, loading skeletons while the AI "thinks," and clear separation between user and AI bubbles.

3.  **Technical Implementation**:
    *   Uses `gemini-3-flash-preview` for low-latency, high-quality reasoning.
    *   Leverages the `ai.chats.create` session management to maintain conversational context throughout the session.
    *   Implements **Streaming Responses** (`sendMessageStream`) to provide immediate visual feedback to the user.

4.  **Security & Privacy**:
    *   The prompt injection ensures the model remains "grounded" in the business's specific data, preventing hallucinations about competitors or generic internet facts.

entries.description: Implement the AI Chat Drawer and contextual assistant logic within the BI module.
