payload.text: Na conta do cliente, ao logar pela primeira vez, deve-se aparecer um menu suspenso para alterar a senha de forma obrigatoria, não podendo fechar a aba até que se altere a senha da conta, fora essa alteração, a unica conta que pode alterar a conta de um cliente, é a do ADM Master, atraves dos clientes já cadastrados

**Analyzing First Login Flow**

I'm currently focused on the first-time login process for this user. My primary concern is ensuring a mandatory password change. I've broken it down to three key constraints: showing the password change modal, preventing closure until the change is made, and applying this specifically to the `ADM_MASTER` role. I'm aiming for a seamless, secure user experience.


**Implementing Password Change Flow**

I'm now zeroing in on the mechanics of the password reset/change functionality. The `User` interface's `mustChangePassword` property is key. I'll integrate UI logic to manage this, focusing on a full-screen modal in `App.tsx` post-authentication. `dataStore.ts` needs verification that `createAccountManual` sets this flag correctly. UserProfile and AdminPanel will be revisited to ensure consistency. A `MandatoryPasswordChange` component will block app usage until a password is set, and upon successful change, update the data store.

Para implementar essa funcionalidade de segurança, realizarei as seguintes atualizações:

1.  **`App.tsx`**: Inclusão de um componente de "Bloqueio de Troca de Senha" que verifica se o usuário logado possui a flag `mustChangePassword` ativa. Se sim, ele sobrepõe toda a aplicação com um formulário inabalável.
2.  **`services/dataStore.ts`**: Adição de um método específico para concluir essa troca obrigatória e desativar a flag de segurança.
3.  **`components/UserProfile.tsx`**: Ajuste para garantir que a interface de perfil respeite as regras de permissão.

Aqui estão as alterações:

entries.description: Adição do método para finalizar a troca de senha obrigatória e resetar a flag de segurança.

payload.text: Quando o cliente logar  pela primera vez na sua conta, deve-se aparecer um menus suspenso como obrigatoriedade de alteração de senha, onde o novo cliente só conseguira acessar o sistema após alterar e confirar a nova senha

**Defining the First Login**

I'm now focused on the initial login experience for new business clients, specifically the mandatory password change. I'm thinking through the need for a modal or overlay for a smoother process. This will ensure their first interaction clearly involves updating their credentials before proceeding.


**Implementing the Lock**

I've moved on to locking down the app until the password is changed. My current thinking revolves around a `MandatoryPasswordChange` component that's inescapable until the user updates their credentials. I'm exploring how to use a `mustChangePassword` flag (already present!) to trigger this UI and prevent access to other areas. I'm also ensuring the password update logic clears the flag properly.


**Clarifying Password Controls**

Now I'm diving deeper into password management restrictions after the initial login. I'm interpreting that, after the mandatory first change, regular users *won't* have access to modify their own passwords within the `UserProfile` section. Only the ADM Master account, and the user during the first-time login process, can initiate password updates. I'm confirming this is a core requirement of the design and exploring the UI implications.


**Refining Mandatory Changes**

I'm now focusing on how the Admin Panel will be able to manage user passwords. The `completeMandatoryPasswordChange(userId, newPass)` function is a key element that needs thorough implementation. I need to confirm its robust operation within `services/dataStore.ts`. I also need to verify and refine `updatePassword(userId, currentPass, newPass)` to ensure it aligns with the updated security rules. This includes handling the UI updates in `App.tsx` via the `mustChangePassword` flag and ensuring the "Alterar Senha" section is disabled/hidden in `components/UserProfile.tsx` for regular users, if the prompt is followed literally. I'm prioritizing the backend implementation first, then the frontend UI. I'm also planning the full-screen backdrop UI, including validation, and datastore action integration.

Para implementar essa camada de segurança obrigatória, realizei as seguintes atualizações:

1.  **`services/dataStore.ts`**: Adicionei o método `updatePassword` (que estava faltando ou incompleto) e garanti que a lógica de troca de senha respeite as flags de segurança.
2.  **`App.tsx`**: Implementei o componente `MandatoryPasswordChange`. Este componente age como um "gate" (portão) que bloqueia o acesso a qualquer outra parte do sistema se o usuário logado tiver a flag `mustChangePassword` como `true`. O usuário não consegue fechar ou navegar sem concluir a ação.
3.  **`components/UserProfile.tsx`**: Removi a seção de alteração de senha para usuários que não sejam **ADM Master**, cumprindo o requisito de que apenas o Master (ou o processo de primeiro login) pode gerenciar as credenciais.

entries.description: Implementação do método de troca de senha e validação de segurança para primeiro login.
Inclusão do componente MandatoryPasswordChange que bloqueia o acesso ao sistema até que a senha seja alterada no primeiro login.
Remoção da seção de alteração de senha para usuários não-master, restringindo o controle de credenciais conforme o requisito.

diagnostics.message: Property 'logo' is missing in type '{ id: string; trackingCode: any; name: any; address: any; cnpj: any; employees: any; city: any; phone: any; email: any; category: any; plan: any; experienceScore: number; monthlyValue: number; implementationValue: number; status: "ACTIVE"; maxEvaluations: number; maxUsers: number; startDate: string; }' but required in type 'Company'.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Missing Property**

I'm currently focused on a TypeScript error within `services/dataStore.ts`. Specifically, a `Company` type is expecting a `logo` property that's missing from the object being passed. I'm zeroing in on the `createAccountManual` function around line 111 to pinpoint where the discrepancy lies and identify the source of the missing `logo` property to rectify this type mismatch.


**Adding Default Logo**

I've determined the root cause: the `Company` type requires a `logo` property, but it's not present when the object is created. I need to add a default value for the `logo` field within the `createAccountManual` function. I'm thinking of using a placeholder image service to provide a temporary URL for the `logo` property.


**Implementing Default Logo URL**

I've decided to integrate a placeholder image URL to the `logo` property. I am going to modify `services/dataStore.ts`. In the `createAccountManual` function, I will add `logo: 'https://picsum.photos/seed/default/200'` to the `company` object. This ensures the `Company` type always has a `logo` value.


**Refining Logo Implementation**

I'm now refining the logo implementation within `dataStore.ts`. I've decided to incorporate a dynamic placeholder image URL for the `logo` property. I will modify the function to add `logo: 'https://picsum.photos/seed/company-' + cid + '/200',` to the object. However, I want to ensure I'm not overwriting an existing logo. I've re-examined the component that calls the function and verified no logo is being passed. I am going to make sure to add it in `dataStore.ts`.


**Reviewing Mock Data**

I'm now diving into the mock data and types to ensure consistency. I'm focusing on the `DataStore` class and its initialization to confirm everything aligns with the `Company` type and the `logo` property. I'm carefully reviewing the mock `MOCK_COMPANIES` and `MOCK_USERS` data to ensure the `logo` is handled as expected within the simulation.


**Generating Feedback Data**

I'm currently working on generating feedback data within the `DataStore` class. I've focused on adding both old and recent feedbacks with varied customer details, including names, phone numbers, how they found out about the business, scores, profiles, and observations. I'm leveraging a loop to create several instances, incorporating data into an array of objects.


**Adding Admin Logs**

I've been adding admin log functionality to `DataStore` to track key actions. I'm focusing on `adminUpdateCompany` and `adminChangeUserRole` functions. I'm adding `addAdminLog` function to track updates and role changes, including details like who made the change, the target object, and the change that was made. I am also adding a function to get all of the admin logs.



**Implementing Password Reset**

I've implemented the `adminResetPassword` function within the `DataStore` class, enabling password resets for users. I'm focusing on ensuring proper logging of these actions to maintain an audit trail. I am tracking the reset event details, including the admin who initiated the reset, the user's ID, name, and a descriptive log message. I'm focusing on handling edge cases, such as the case where the user is not found.


**Finalizing Account Provisioning**

I've just finalized the account provisioning process within `createAccountManual`. I've added a default `logo` using `https://picsum.photos/seed/company-${cid}/200`. I have defined other default properties, such as `experienceScore`, `monthlyValue`, `implementationValue`, `status`, `maxEvaluations`, `maxUsers`, and `startDate`. I have added an admin log for the account creation. Now, I will move on to the next function.


**Refactoring Password Updates**

I'm currently refactoring `updatePassword` and `resetPassword`. I've extracted the core password update logic, and I'm ensuring consistency by calling an internal `_updateUserPassword` method to centralize the password update process and reduce code duplication, while managing the password change flag and onboarding status within the update process. I am focusing on streamlining the `adminProvisionGuardian` function.


**Linking Guardians & Companies**

I'm now implementing `adminLinkGuardianToCompany` and `adminUnlinkGuardianFromCompany` functions. I'm focusing on ensuring guardians can be linked and unlinked to companies, updating the `linkedCompanyIds` array and adding appropriate admin logs. I'm also addressing edge cases like missing `linkedCompanyIds` arrays.


**Adding Collaborator Removal**

I'm now implementing the `adminRemoveCollaborator` functionality to completely remove users from the system. I'm focusing on user removal logic within `adminRemoveCollaborator`, ensuring appropriate admin logs are generated. I am also planning to refactor the `adminRemoveCollaborator` and `adminUpdateCompany` and `adminChangeUserRole` functions.

I am working on the function `adminRemoveCollaborator`. I want to ensure the appropriate admin logs are generated. I'm also preparing to refactor `adminRemoveCollaborator`, as well as `adminUpdateCompany` and `adminChangeUserRole`, to maintain consistency in the functions. I'm also planning to refactor `adminRemoveCollaborator`, to make sure that the code is readable.


**Refining Functionality**

I'm now refining the `updateCompany`, `addCompanyUser` and `updateUser` functions. I'm taking another look at the `updateCompany` function to make sure that the company object can be updated properly. I am considering edge cases, specifically the behavior when the company isn't found. I'm also ensuring the new user added to the `addCompanyUser` function gets properly set up as a user. I'm also making sure that the existing user can be properly updated in the `updateUser` function.



**Calculating Customer Categorization**

I'm currently working on customer categorization within the feedback processing. I've implemented logic to calculate and update a customer's `averageScore` based on their feedback, and I've also incorporated the logic to categorize them as 'PROMOTER', 'DETRACTOR', or 'PASSIVE' based on their score. I'm focusing on ensuring this logic accurately updates the `customer.category` property within the `DataStore` class.

I've just focused on refining the feedback integration within the `DataStore`. I'm updating the customer's average score and category based on incoming feedback. The `averageScore` is being recalculated and updated, and the customer is being categorized as "PROMOTER", "DETRACTOR", or "PASSIVE". I'm calculating the total number of feedback items, the count of promoters, detractors, and passives, and the average score. I'm also calculating open and resolved detractors and category averages.

payload.text: ao acessar o primeiro login (conta de contratante) de uma conta recem criada pelo ADM Master, quando atualiazo a senha em ``Segurança obrigatoria``, fica carregando e não vai para o sistema, preciso que ao apertar para confirmar a nova senha, o sistema carregue para a tela de ``dashboard`` e a senha colocada pelo cliente, atualizer como a nova senha da conta

**Analyzing Login Flow**

I'm currently investigating a stubborn "Mandatory Password Change" loop in our app. It seems that after a user creates an account and updates their password, the expected redirection to the dashboard fails. I'm focusing on the login flow and authentication process to pinpoint the exact point of divergence. I'm now testing different scenarios, and gathering logging data, to see if the server authentication is getting properly signaled back to the client app.


**Pinpointing The Bug**

I've traced the flow from the `MandatoryPasswordChange` component in `.tsx` to the `completeMandatoryPasswordChange` function in `dataStore.ts`. The `onComplete` callback in `App.tsx` should update the `user` state after a successful password change. I've confirmed that the conditional rendering in `App.tsx` correctly checks the `mustChangePassword` flag. Now I'm examining `dataStore.ts` and following the data transformations through the update process, specifically the user property updates.


**Investigating Data Flow**

I'm now zeroing in on the interaction between `handleSubmit` within the `MandatoryPasswordChange` component in `App.tsx` and the `onComplete` callback. It looks like a potential issue might be with the way the `user` state updates after the `completeMandatoryPasswordChange` function in `dataStore.ts` returns. I'm suspecting a mismatch between object references and a possible lack of trigger for a re-render. I'll focus on whether the `updated` data changes the object reference in the `user` object.


**Refining Object Updates**

I've realized the `dataStore` methods currently mutate and return the *same* user object. In React, this might not trigger a re-render. To fix this, I need to modify `dataStore.ts` to return *cloned* user objects using the spread operator (`{...user}`) in `completeMandatoryPasswordChange`, and other user-updating methods. I'm also confirming the redirect to the dashboard should happen automatically. I'm updating `dataStore.ts` and will then double-check `App.tsx`'s `onComplete` handling.

Este problema ocorre porque o React muitas vezes não detecta a mudança de estado se o objeto retornado tiver a mesma referência na memória, mesmo que seus valores internos tenham mudado. 

Para resolver isso e garantir que o sistema redirecione instantaneamente para o dashboard após a troca de senha, farei as seguintes correções:

1.  **`services/dataStore.ts`**: Atualizarei os métodos para retornarem uma **cópia (clone)** do objeto de usuário. Isso força o React a reconhecer que houve uma alteração de estado.
2.  **`App.tsx`**: Garantirei que o componente de troca de senha limpe o estado de carregamento e force a atualização da interface.

entries.description: Ajuste nos métodos de atualização de usuário para retornar novas referências de objeto (clones), garantindo que o React detecte a mudança de estado e desmonte o bloqueio de senha.
Correção na callback de conclusão da troca de senha obrigatória para garantir que o componente de carregamento seja fechado e o sistema atualize o estado do usuário corretamente.

payload.text:
Na landing page, retire a aba ``Planos``.

Não retire nada além da aba da landing page! Os planos continuam existindo, mas não serão visiveis no site

**Removing the "Plans" Tab**

Okay, I'm working on removing the "Planos" tab from the landing page. I've located the navigation items within the `Header` component in `LandingPage.tsx`. My current focus is on pinpointing the exact entry for "Planos" within either `NAV_ITEMS` or hardcoded navigation to excise it effectively. Then, I will confirm the component is displaying as expected.


**Eliminating the "Planos" Link**

I've successfully identified the "Planos" button within the `Header` component in `LandingPage.tsx` and the corresponding entry in the `Footer` component. I'm proceeding to remove both, and the view will be kept. I'll make sure the `PlanosView` logic stays intact and accessible internally, but not via the user interface.


**Isolating the "Planos" View**

I'm now implementing the removal of the direct "Planos" access points, as requested. I've pinpointed the `onClick` event listeners in both `Header` and `Footer` components of `LandingPage.tsx` that trigger the display of the `PLANOS` view, which I will remove. I'm verifying the `PLANOS` view itself, including the `PriceCard` and table, will remain intact, albeit inaccessible through user navigation. The specific prompt instruction prevents further code removal.

Para atender ao seu pedido de forma precisa, mantendo a integridade de todas as outras funcionalidades e planos do sistema, mas ocultando apenas o acesso visual a eles na landing page, farei as seguintes alterações no arquivo `components/LandingPage.tsx`:

1.  **Header**: Removi o botão "Planos" da navegação principal.
2.  **Footer**: Removi o link "Planos" da lista de navegação do rodapé.

Os planos continuam existindo no código e nas outras telas do sistema (como faturamento e administração), apenas o acesso via Landing Page foi removido conforme solicitado.

entries.description: Remoção da aba e links de 'Planos' apenas na interface da Landing Page, mantendo a lógica interna intacta.

payload.text: Na conta de ADM Master, na aba console ADM, quero que ao clicar em um lead, eu consiga colocar uma tag de ``consultoria Guardiã``, essa tag faz com que a conta guardiã tenha acesso a o dash board, avaliações, nps, casos, formularios das contas com essa tag

**Defining Access Criteria**

I'm currently focused on how to best implement the "Consultoria Guardiã" tag within the `MasterAdminPanel`. My central aim is to ensure that only users with the `GUARDIAN` role can access companies with this tag. I'm exploring the most efficient method to connect this access control to the company's data.


**Refining Guardian Access**

I've made progress adding `isGuardianConsultancy` to the `Company` type. Next, I'll integrate `adminToggleGuardianConsultancy` in the `dataStore`. The core challenge is integrating this toggle logic for the Guardian role. I'll need to modify the data store's company retrieval method to respect this new property. The `Layout.tsx` already has access to accessible companies. I'll prioritize ensuring Guardians see the tagged companies.


**Implementing Toggle UI**

I'm now focused on implementing the toggle UI within the `DETAILS` modal of the company in the admin panel. I'll integrate this with the `dataStore` for `isGuardianConsultancy`. Next, I will need to update `Layout.tsx`'s access filter, so that Guardians can see tagged companies. Updating `types.ts` is also on the docket, and implementing toggle logic in `services/dataStore.ts`.

O recurso de **Consultoria Guardiã** é uma funcionalidade estratégica poderosa. Ao ativar esta "tag" em uma empresa, você abre um canal de visualização global para todos os usuários com o perfil **Guardião**, permitindo que eles prestem consultoria baseada em dados sem a necessidade de vínculos manuais um a um.

Aqui está a especificação técnica das alterações:

1.  **`types.ts`**: Adição da propriedade `isGuardianConsultancy` à interface `Company`.
2.  **`services/dataStore.ts`**: Implementação do método `adminToggleGuardianConsultancy` para gerenciar a tag e registro de log de auditoria.
3.  **`components/MasterAdminPanel.tsx`**: Inclusão de um novo card de controle dentro do modal de detalhes da empresa para ativar/desativar a Consultoria Guardiã com uma identidade visual distinta (Roxo/Indigo).
4.  **`components/Layout.tsx`**: Atualização da lógica de roteamento de permissões. Agora, usuários **Guardiões** enxergam automaticamente no seletor de unidades todas as empresas que possuem a tag ativa, além das que já estão vinculadas diretamente a eles.

entries.description: Adição da flag isGuardianConsultancy para permitir acesso global a consultores.
Implementação do método para alternar a tag de Consultoria Guardiã.
Atualização da lógica de visibilidade para usuários Guardiões incluirem empresas com Consultoria Guardiã ativa.
Inclusão da tag 'Consultoria Guardiã' no Console ADM para permitir acesso global de guardiões às métricas da empresa.
